mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X4 35d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned X4 35d engine (N57D30B) typically costs £2,800–£4,200 supply only. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500. A full engine replacement with labour can cost £7,500–£10,500. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X4 35d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N57 is a strong, torquey engine capable of high mileage – well‑serviced examples can exceed 180,000 miles. However, it has well‑documented weaknesses: timing chain wear, oil leaks (valve cover, oil filter housing), EGR cooler failure, and low oil pressure concerns. Regular oil changes (8,000–10,000 miles) are essential – BMW advised police fleets to shorten oil change intervals to 10,000 miles.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X4 35d?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most common issues are timing chain stretch and guide failure – the chain, guides, and tensioners can wear prematurely. Other common problems include oil leaks from the valve cover and oil filter housing, EGR cooler failure, and low oil pressure.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an X4 35d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a tidy £8,000–£18,000 X4 35d, a £2,800–£4,800 recon is 15–40% of the car's value – often worth it on well‑maintained examples. If the timing chain has snapped, a full engine replacement may be necessary. Get a full diagnostic assessment before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my X4's engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The X4 35d (F26, 2014–2018) uses the N57D30B (also N57D30T1 or N57D30TOP) – a 3.0‑litre twin‑turbo diesel inline-six. The engine code is stamped on the engine block and listed on your V5C. The 35d badge itself confirms the engine – it's the 313 PS twin‑turbo diesel flagship.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair an X4 35d engine issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most issues – timing chain wear (if caught early), oil leaks, EGR cooler failure – are repairable. However, if the timing chain has snapped or the engine has suffered catastrophic oil pressure failure, a full engine replacement is usually more cost‑effective than a rebuild. A low oil pressure warning should never be ignored – stop driving immediately.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the F26 X4 35d generation. The N57D30B is a complex twin‑turbo engine — replacement costs reflect the higher labour and parts requirements of a six‑cylinder diesel. A full engine replacement with labour can cost £7,500–£10,500.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an X4 35d engine typically takes 12‑16 hours at £75‑£110/hr (independent BMW specialist). The twin‑turbo setup and X4's engine bay layout add complexity.

cta.label: → Compare X4 35d engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: N57 Timing Chain Stretch & Guide Failure
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise during cold starts, metallic ticking from the engine, rattling persisting when warm, engine warning light (cam timing faults), loss of power — catastrophic failure can occur without warning.
problems.replacementCost: £2,800–£4,800 [PLACEHOLDER] (if chain snaps and damages the engine).

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling or ticking, book an inspection immediately.

problems.recommendation: The N57's timing chain is a known weak point. The chain, guides, and tensioners can wear prematurely, causing chain stretch. If the chain stretches too far, it can "jump" a tooth or snap — in this interference engine, that means piston‑to‑valve contact and a very expensive repair. A £1,500–£3,000 preventative chain replacement is far cheaper than a £2,800–£4,800 engine replacement. At the first sign of cold‑start rattle, have the timing system inspected immediately. Well‑serviced N57 engines with regular oil changes can exceed 180,000 miles.

problems.title: Oil Leaks — Valve Cover & Oil Filter Housing (N57)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the vehicle, burning oil smell, low oil level warning, visible oil leak from the top or front of the engine.
problems.repairCost: £300–£900 [PLACEHOLDER] (valve cover gasket or oil filter housing gasket replacement).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored oil loss can lead to engine damage).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if oil leak is detected.

problems.recommendation: Oil leaks from the valve cover and oil filter housing are common on N57 engines. A leaking valve cover gasket can allow oil to drip onto the exhaust manifold, causing a burning oil smell and potential fire risk. The oil filter housing gasket is also prone to failure — if it fails, oil can leak onto the serpentine belt, causing it to slip or break, which can then be ingested into the engine through the front crankshaft seal, causing catastrophic failure. A £300–£900 gasket replacement is far cheaper than the consequences of a snapped belt. Regular oil level checks are essential.

problems.title: EGR Cooler Failure (N57)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss without visible external leaks, white smoke from exhaust, engine warning light, rough idle, loss of power.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored coolant loss can cause engine damage).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant loss or white smoke occurs.

problems.recommendation: EGR cooler failure is a known issue on N57 engines. The EGR cooler can develop leaks, allowing coolant to enter the exhaust system, causing white smoke and coolant loss. If ignored, coolant loss can lead to overheating and potential engine damage. Regular coolant level checks are essential — if you notice coolant loss with no visible external leak, have the EGR system inspected promptly.

problems.title: Low Oil Pressure (N57)
problems.symptoms: Oil pressure warning light, ticking or knocking from the engine, loss of power, eventual engine failure.
problems.replacementCost: £2,800–£4,800 [PLACEHOLDER] (if oil pressure loss causes catastrophic failure).

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if the oil pressure warning light comes on, stop driving immediately.

problems.recommendation: Low oil pressure is a serious concern on N57 engines — oil pressure below 1.2‑1.5 bar at idle is considered critical. Low oil pressure can be caused by a failing oil pump, clogged oil pickup, or worn bearings. If the oil pressure warning light comes on, stop driving immediately — continued driving will cause catastrophic engine failure. Regular oil changes (using the correct BMW LL‑spec oil) and maintaining correct oil levels are the best preventative measures. BMW advised police fleets using N57 models to shorten oil change intervals to 10,000 miles.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW X4 35d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch it early — cheaper than engine replacement
Full engine replacement — valve damage is almost certain
Worth fixing — common and well‑understood repair
Depends on damage extent — may require full rebuild or replacement
Full engine replacement often more cost‑effective

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW X4 35d? Check These First
buyIf: * Full BMW service history with documented oil changes (every 8,000–10,000 miles)
* Timing chain inspected and any work documented — this is critical on higher‑mileage cars
* Evidence of regular oil level monitoring and top‑ups
* A well‑maintained example with lower mileage
avoidIf: * Any sign of cold‑start rattle — timing chain warning
* Any sign of oil leaks — valve cover or oil filter housing issues
* Oil pressure warning light or low oil level warnings on the dashboard
* Poor service history — the N57 is intolerant of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The BMW X4 35d represents the pinnacle of first‑generation X4 diesel performance — a twin‑turbo straight‑six with 313 PS and 630 Nm of effortless torque. The N57 engine is a strong, torquey unit capable of high mileage with proper maintenance, but it has well‑documented weaknesses: timing chain wear, oil leaks, EGR cooler failure, and low oil pressure concerns. Buy on service history above all else — a vehicle with documented timing chain maintenance and regular oil changes is a safe bet; a neglected example can be expensive to repair. As one specialist noted, "well-serviced N57 engines can run well over 180,000 miles".

cta.label: → Compare X4 35d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X4 35d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Cold Start — Any rattle or ticking? (N57 timing chain warning — stop immediately)
2. Service History — Regular oil changes (8,000–10,000 miles) — this is non‑negotiable
3. Timing Chain History — Has the chain been inspected or replaced?
4. Oil Leaks — Check under the vehicle and around the valve cover and oil filter housing
5. Oil Pressure — Any oil pressure warning lights? Any low oil level warnings?
6. Coolant Level — Any sign of coolant loss? (EGR cooler warning)
7. White Smoke — Any white smoke from the exhaust? (EGR cooler failure)
8. MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures or any advisory notes

engineCodes.matchNote: This table's rows match Section 1B's Era Map exactly.
